tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine and chloride, significant proteinuria, ketonuria, and glucosuria, with low urine specific gravity and brown, cloudy urine. HbA1c is low, suggesting no chronic hyperglycemia. Findings indicate possible acute or chronic kidney dysfunction with impaired filtration, and possible tubular damage or acute illness. Urine changes may also suggest dehydration or urinary tract pathology. Clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
